nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coastal reef zones and lagoons in the photic zone; genus-typical shallow subtidal habitat on reef substrate

decorPreferences: loose placement on rockwork or rubble

avoidWith: heavily grazing herbivores that may consume the specimen before it establishes

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product image from Mosaic Macros listing for Flat-Branched Striped Gracilaria (Gracilaria mammaralis)

summary: A red macroalgae with distinctive flat, plate-like branching and striped patterning. Available primarily through Mosaic Macros; tends to grow well under standard Gracilaria refugium conditions.
careNotes:
Gracilaria mammaralis, sold by Mosaic Macros as 'Flat-Branched Striped Gracilaria', is a red macroalgae (Rhodophyta) in the family Gracilariaceae, order Gracilariales — the same family as other commonly kept Gracilaria species in the hobby. Its distinguishing feature is a flat, plate-like branching growth form with striped patterning that develops as the specimen matures, in contrast to the rounder, more cylindrical branching typical of species like Red Ogo (Gracilaria parvispora) or the bushy ball-form of Pom Pom (Gracilaria hayi).

As a Mosaic Macros specialty offering, dedicated husbandry documentation for this specific species is sparse. Water parameters are extrapolated from family-level Gracilaria care and confirmed across sibling species in the genus: temperature is generally advised in the range of 22–26°C (72–78°F), salinity 1.023–1.025 SG, pH 8.1–8.4, and alkalinity 8–12 dKH — standard reef parameters suitable for all Gracilariaceae species in the trade.

Lighting is expected to be medium intensity, consistent with other Gracilaria refugium species. Growth rate is anticipated to be fast, in keeping with the genus. The flat branching form suggests it may be more decorative than purely utilitarian compared to tumbling refugium species; however, like other Gracilaria, it can be expected to provide meaningful nitrate and phosphate export when grown under adequate light and flow.

Propagation is by simple fragmentation, the standard method for all aquarium Gracilaria. Cuttings that detach or are trimmed will generally form new growth points when returned to appropriate conditions.

As with all Gracilaria, herbivorous fish such as tangs and rabbitfish are likely to graze it. Whether this is desirable (as supplemental live food) or a problem (for display specimens) depends on system goals. No copper-based medications should be used in systems containing this or any macroalgae.

Mosaic Macros is the sole documented hobby source for this species at time of research. Family-level care parameters from sibling species (Red Ogo sw-algae-007, Pom Pom sw-algae-006, Thick Ogo sw-algae-003, Green Ogo sw-algae-013, Red Spaghetti sw-algae-015, Monkey Finger sw-algae-014) were used to fill numeric parameters; those values are well-established for the genus. AlgaeBase returned a human-verification block and could not confirm the species entry directly, but Gracilariaceae family placement is confirmed by the scientific name and consistent with all trade-source references. Per Mosaic care guide guidance: as a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed acceptance per Mosaic is limited to Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea genera, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)
breedingNotes: In captivity, propagation is achieved through simple fragmentation: portions of the thallus that detach or are trimmed will generally establish new growth when placed in adequate flow and lighting. Sexual reproduction via alternation of gametophyte and sporophyte generations occurs in the wild but is not a documented propagation path in the hobby.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby source. Confirms scientific name Gracilaria mammaralis and flat plate-like branching growth form with striped patterning. No water parameter or numeric husbandry data provided on product page.

additional.name: Sibling species cross-reference (Red Ogo, Pom Pom, Gracilariaceae genus-level)
additional.notes: Water parameters (temperatureC, pH, kH, salinity) extrapolated from established sibling-species data for Gracilaria parvispora (sw-algae-007) and Gracilaria hayi (sw-algae-006), both Gracilariaceae. Parameters are standard reef values confirmed across multiple trade sources for the genus.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
